subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Comprehensive and clear table of contents enabling easy navigation
Well-structured sections with logical introductory, methodological and concluding flow
Explicit statement of aims and appropriate use of headings and sub-headings
weaknesses: Occasional algebraic digressions (lengthy logarithm manipulation) interrupt coherence
Verbose background detail detracts from conciseness
Minor lapses in page numbering and relocation of equations prevent polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation demonstrates strong organization and logical development, supported by a clear investigative sequence and well-formatted structure. While mostly concise and relevant, occasional digressions and verbose sections limit it to a band-3 performance rather than the exceptionally concise, fully polished quality required for band 4.

strengths: Step-by-step derivations accompanied by explanatory commentary for clarity
Mostly consistent and appropriate mathematical notation (Ï, RÂ², dy/dx)
Effective use of multiple representations (GeoGebra graphs, tables, algebraic expressions)
weaknesses: Formatting glitches and mid-calculation asides cloud clarity at points
Misnotation in EQ2 and unlabelled axes on imported graphs hinder precise interpretation
Axes and units missing on some figures reduce overall readability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student communicates the mathematics clearly with logical structure and consistent notation. Multiple representations strengthen understanding, though occasional notation errors, unlabeled graphs and minor formatting issues limit the work to a solid band-3 rather than the highly consistent clarity of band 4.

strengths: Personal context (succulents and watering devices) engages reader and shows student interest
Noticeable independent thinking in selecting and modelling a household object
Good creativity and initiative in researching volume of revolution beyond standard syllabus
weaknesses: Approach mirrors common IA exemplars, limiting distinctiveness
Pragmatic switch to spreadsheet fitting reduces demonstration of manual initiative
Personal innovation is clear but not sustained at an exceptional level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration reflects a clear personal approach and independent thinking, with creative choices in modelling. However, reliance on standard procedures and the decision to abandon deeper manual work constrain this to a band-2 performance rather than the highly innovative work of band 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on measurement error, software limitations and model accuracy
Thoughtful analysis of RÂ² values and recognition of how point selection influences results
Clear evaluation of outcomes evidenced by the calculated 9.3% error and suggested improvements
weaknesses: Reflection is brief and largely descriptive rather than critically probing assumptions
Limited depth in discussing modelling assumptions and alternatives
Future implications are mentioned only superficially without concrete extension ideas
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides thoughtful, meaningful reflection with clear outcome evaluation, meriting band-2. To reach band-3, more substantial critical reflection of underlying assumptions and comprehensive forward-looking implications would be required.

strengths: Mathematics directly and effectively applied to volume determination (coordinate capture, derivation, integration)
Techniques (circle/ellipse properties, regression, volume of revolution) are fully commensurate with SL
Clear simultaneous equation steps in linear regression reflect solid understanding
weaknesses: Minor algebraic and transcription errors (mis-typed formula, inconsistent integration limits)
Rounding intermediate constants introduces small propagation errors
Omitted detailed numeric integration steps reduce transparency of final results
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work employs relevant and appropriate SL-level mathematics effectively, demonstrating good command of calculus and modelling techniques. Minor errors and inconsistencies limit the understanding and accuracy to band-4 rather than the thorough, error-free performance of higher bands.

strandSelections.reasoning: Each derivation is set out step-by-step and followed by commentary, giving the reader a clear path. Occasional formatting glitches and mid-calculation digressions slightly cloud clarity, but **overall logical communication is solid**.

Derivations generally show good understanding, and numerical results are plausible; yet there are minor algebraic slips (e.g. mis-typed circle formula, inconsistent upper limit 10.7077 vs 10.9899) and the exponential integration contains sign/constant errors later corrected numerically. Hence understanding is **good but not thorough or completely correct**.

The student employs algebraic expressions, graphs from GeoGebra, Google Sheets plots and tabulated data. These multiple representations **communicate the modelling process clearly**; however, scales and axes on some imported images are small and not always labelled, so the use is effective rather than highly effective.

**The report follows a clear investigative sequence** (introduction â methodology â exploration â evaluation) and each subsection logically builds on the previous one. Headings, sub-headings and the table of contents make navigation easy, and transitions (e.g. âtherefore, my next stepâ¦â) show purposeful flow. However, some algebraic digressions (e.g. lengthy logarithm manipulation later abandoned) momentarily interrupt the argument, so the logical thread is good rather than exemplary.

The decision to split the object, use software to capture points, and derive functions shows **reasonable creativity and initiative**. Yet the exponential function was ultimately generated by spreadsheet fitting after an abandoned manual attempt, limiting the sense of exceptional initiative.

There is thoughtful analysis of RÂ² values and recognition that point selection and exponential fitting influence results, indicating **thoughtful but not deep critical analysis**. The exploration stops short of re-modelling or quantitatively testing alternative fits.

The student evaluates outcome accuracy via percentage error and suggests improvements (use of automated point capture, refining the exponential), providing a **clear evaluation of outcomes**. Future implications are mentioned only briefly, so the evaluation is adequate rather than comprehensive.

The student reflects on measurement error, software limitations and model accuracy (9.3 % error) â this constitutes **meaningful reflection**. Reflection is, however, brief and mostly descriptive rather than critically probing modelling assumptions.

Variables, parameters and integral limits are usually defined and correct notation (Ï, RÂ², dy/dx) is used almost consistently. A few slips occur (EQ 2 shows âx â yââ instead of ây â yââ, some misplaced subscripts), but overall mathematical language is **good and mostly consistent**.

The exploration is clearly framed around the studentâs interest in succulents and watering devices, demonstrating a **personal approach**. Nevertheless, the modelling strategy mirrors common IA exemplars, so the approach, while evident, is not highly distinctive.

Most material is relevant to determining volume, but the narrative contains repeated definitions (ellipse and circle) and verbose step-by-step algebra that could have been summarised. The write-up reaches 20 pages plus lengthy raw data tables, so **conciseness is only moderate**.

All mathematics directly supports volume determination: coordinate capture, equation derivation, definite integration. Each part is **clearly relevant and effectively applied**; there is no superfluous calculus unrelated to the aim.

Choosing a personal household object, designing piecewise models and deciding to test accuracy against real volume shows **noticeable independent thinking**, yet most techniques (ellipse-to-circle transform, RÂ² check) follow standard textbook or software procedures.

Sections are well ordered, numbered and referenced; figures, tables and appendices are cited in the text. The investigation opens with context and closes with conclusions, giving the work a recognisable structure. **Minor lapses** appear in page numbering (appendices exceed declared length) and occasional relocation of equations, preventing the exceptionally polished quality required for band 4.

Circle/ellipse properties, exponential and linear regression, and volume of revolution are at or slightly above SL level (rotation is HL but acceptable as extension). Techniques are **fully commensurate with SL** and not trivial.

annotations.comment: The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly structured, enabling easy navigation between sections and demonstrating strong organizational skills.
The introductory context is engaging and personal, linking the investigation to your interest in succulents and demonstrating clear personal engagement.
The introduction contains lengthy background detail that, while interesting, detracts from conciseness. Consider summarizing the botanical context more succinctly.
The methodology section shows initiative in researching volume of revolution beyond the syllabus, reflecting independent thinking and extra effort.
Equation (EQ2) misstates the circle: the second term should read \((y - y_0)^2\) rather than \((x - y_0)^2\). Correct notation is crucial for clarity.
The explanation of volume of revolution (EQ3) is clear, but including a brief derivation or reference to axis rotation would strengthen comprehension.
The step showing expansion of \(y^2\) before integration is logical and sets up the calculus clearly, aiding reader comprehension.
The tail integral bounds and substitution are consistent, but the final numeric integration steps are omitted; include these to verify the 26.0 cmÂ³ result.
The evaluation section outlines limitations and suggests improvements, but deeper critical reflection on modelling assumptions would strengthen insight.
The conclusion reiterates results but offers limited forward-looking implications; consider suggesting specific future investigations or extensions.
References are appropriately formatted and comprehensive, demonstrating thorough research and citation practice.
Appendix tables contain raw data but exceed declared page count; consider summarizing or moving detailed tables to supplementary materials.
The final aim is explicitly stated and well-aligned with the research question, providing clear focus for the exploration.
The piecewise function domain values are later inconsistently applied (10.9899 vs 10.7077). Verify domain endpoints to ensure accurate integration limits.
Defining the piecewise function formally is effective. You clearly indicate subdomains, aiding logical structure and clarity.
Rounding intermediate constants (e.g. 5.25^2 = 27.56) introduces small errors; carry extra precision until final steps to avoid propagation.
Good choice to select only the positive branch of the circle, reinforcing clarity that y is nonnegative on this interval.
Seven sample points effectively illustrate model accuracy. Labeling axes and scales on these graphs would further improve communication.
The transition from circle to ellipse is well justified with clear algebraic steps, showcasing effective multiple representations.
The manual logarithmic solution for the exponential fit is lengthy and interrupted; condense or remove this digression to improve conciseness.
Given the complexity, adopting spreadsheet fitting was pragmatic but reflects limited initiative in pursuing deeper manual solutions.
The Google Sheets exponential trendline is effective, but the graph lacks labelled axes and units, which would enhance readability.
Presenting both manual and software fits for the neck demonstrates thoughtful critical analysis of methods and results.
The linear regression model for the tail is accurately derived, with clear simultaneous equation steps reflecting good mathematical understanding.
Figure 8 illustrating the straight-line fit lacks axis labels and units, hindering precise interpretation of the RÂ² value.
Integration for the head part is computed correctly but the unexpected use of 10.7077 as an upper limit (instead of 10.9899) suggests a transcription error.
Summing modelled volumes and comparing with the measured volume is effective. You correctly calculate a 9.3% error, demonstrating clear evaluation.
Including appendices of point lists demonstrates transparency of data collection, supporting reproducibility of the modelling process.
